title: One pilot task pack end-to-end → mapped, validated, merged
context: The M0 milestone requires proving the full open-map-gaps pipeline end-to-end with one pilot pack that reaches a validated, merged outcome — not just 'pack written, unused.' The pilot is gated on: a named partner or local community who has agreed to map/validate a small area (or, failing that, a self-serve-validatable fallback — a tiny, low-sensitivity area with an active local community and an independent validator). All M0 prerequisite tasks (reviewer-001, imagery-gate-002, harm-gate-003, template-004, tagging-005, outreach-006) must be complete before mapping begins. The effort must be registered as OSMF Organised Editing.
objective: Assemble, execute, and document one complete pilot task pack — from area selection through gate checks, pack assembly, human mapping, validation, and merged outcome — producing all required artifacts.
acceptanceCriteria: Pilot is gated on a realistic validated-merge path: a partner/local community agreed to map and validate a small area, or a self-serve-validatable fallback (low-sensitivity area, active local community, independent validator) is confirmed.
Area passed imagery-gate-002 (authorized imagery + ODbL-compatible sources) and harm-gate-003 (signed) with both artifacts committed.
Pack assembled from template-004 and tagging-005: AoI GeoJSON, tag schema, mapping and validation instructions, attribution, and chosen channel (TM/MapRoulette/Notes).
Effort registered under OSMF Organised Editing with wiki page URL recorded in the pack.

Features mapped by a human and validated and merged into OSM (not reverted); validator sign-off and changeset/TM references recorded in outcomes/<pack-id>.json — or, if no channel materializes, pack fully prepared with the blocker surfaced.
All OSM-derived outputs licensed ODbL-1.0; no OSM write credentials used by any tool.
Outcome artifact records validated-feature count, pass rate, and any verified beneficiary use.
output: A committed pilot task pack (AoI GeoJSON, tagging schema, instructions, gate artifacts, channel artifact) and an outcomes/<pack-id>.json recording the validated-feature count, pass rate, validator sign-off, and changeset/TM references (or a blocker statement if no channel materializes).
